beats.title: Portents and the Fall
beats.boundaryReason: Table talk and the DM's omens-heavy prologue lead into the moment the party awakens on the ice platform.
dateEvidence: session.yaml sets drStart and drEnd to 1730-01-25
the next in-scene beat is explicitly described as taking place around 3 o'clock in the afternoon
no sleep, rest, or next-day cue appears before the awakening

beats.title: Strangers on the Ice
beats.boundaryReason: The party surveys the floating ice platform, introduces themselves, and studies the storm and wolves until Eolo walks up to a guardian and triggers initiative.
dateEvidence: DM says it was 'like, 3 o'clock in the afternoon' before the fall
the platform scene follows immediately from that fall with no sign of a day change

beats.title: Ice Wolves Awaken
beats.boundaryReason: Combat begins when Eolo approaches the wolf statue, and the first phase centers on the wolves' initial rush before the second wave of shadow-creatures fully enters the fight.
dateEvidence: this is the first combat immediately after the 3 PM platform scene
no downtime or day-transition cue interrupts the encounter

beats.title: Shades in the Wind
beats.boundaryReason: A new combat phase starts when a second shade arrives, the shrieking wind attack drops the Zimkovan woman, and the battlefield fills with darkness and additional threats.
dateEvidence: the fight continues without any break on the same date
there is no rest, sleep, sunset, or explicit time advance during this phase

beats.title: Darkness and Final Blows
beats.boundaryReason: After Ekko kills the first wisp of this phase, the remaining wolves, shades, and wisps are cleaned up in a final scramble until the platform falls quiet.
dateEvidence: this remains the same uninterrupted combat sequence on 1730-01-25
the battle ends before any rest or day-transition cue appears

beats.title: Kalima Names the Labyrinth
beats.boundaryReason: With the fight over, Finnan and Justas calm the woman enough to learn that this is Zeyfa's Labyrinth and to hear the first fragments of her people's history and fear.
dateEvidence: this conversation begins immediately after combat ends on the same platform
no rest or next-day transition occurs before Kalima's explanation

beats.title: Stories on the Platform
beats.boundaryReason: The focus shifts from extracting facts from Kalima to the party exchanging introductions, motives, and theories about the chasm, the Kestavo, and what sort of place they have entered.
dateEvidence: the discussion is continuous with Kalima's testimony on the same day
no sleep, waking, or calendar advance is indicated

beats.title: Rest, Recon, and Descent
beats.boundaryReason: The party questions Kalima further, takes a short rest, tests the platform's exits, learns the drop is wind-locked, scouts the snow chamber ahead, persuades Kalima to come, and commits to entering the tunnels.
dateEvidence: DM explicitly says the group may as well take a short rest
a short rest advances time within the same day rather than to a new date
the transcript ends before any sunset, sleep, or next-morning cue
